items.assessment:
1. Lumbar radiculopathy - L4-L5 disc bulge with left L5 nerve root involvement confirmed on MRI
2. Prediabetes - fasting glucose pending follow-up
items.plan:
1. Lumbar MRI reviewed - discussed findings with patient
2. Physical therapy referral for core strengthening and McKenzie protocol
3. Continue naproxen 500mg BID as needed
4. Follow-up fasting glucose for prediabetes monitoring
5. If no improvement in 6-8 weeks of PT, consider epidural steroid injection or pain management referral
6. Follow-up in 6 weeks

items.assessment: Acute low back pain, mechanical - possible disc pathology given mechanism of injury
items.plan:
1. Lumbar spine X-ray ordered to evaluate bony structures
2. Start naproxen 500mg BID with food
3. Activity modification - avoid heavy lifting, gradual return to normal activities
4. Ice for first 48 hours, then alternate heat/ice
5. Return in 6 weeks; if not improving, will order lumbar MRI
6. Red flag precautions discussed - return immediately for leg weakness, bowel/bladder changes
